big5.Agreeableness: the tendency to act in a cooperative, unselfish manner, construed as one end of a dimension of individual differences.
big5.Conscientiousness: the tendency to be organized, responsible, and hardworking, construed as one end of a dimension of individual differences.
big5.Extraversion: an orientation of one's interests and energies toward the outer world of people and things rather than the inner world of subjective experience. Extraverts are relatively outgoing, gregarious, sociable, and openly expressive.
big5.Neuroticism: a chronic level of emotional instability and proneness to psychological distress.
big5.Openness to experience: the tendency to be open to new aesthetic, cultural, or intellectual experiences.

pvq.Achievement: personal success through demonstrating competence according to social standards.
pvq.Benevolence: preserving and enhancing the welfare of those with whom one is in frequent personal contact (the 'in-group').
pvq.Conformity: restraint of actions, inclinations, and impulses likely to upset or harm others and violate social expectations or norms.
pvq.Hedonism: pleasure or sensuous gratification for oneself.
pvq.Power: social status and prestige, control or dominance over people and resources.
pvq.Security: safety, harmony, and stability of society, of relationships, and of self.
pvq.Self-Direction: independent thought and action--choosing, creating, exploring.
pvq.Stimulation: excitement, novelty, and challenge in life.
pvq.Tradition: respect, commitment, and acceptance of the customs and ideas that one's culture or religion provides.
pvq.Universalism: understanding, appreciation, tolerance, and protection for the welfare of all people and for nature.

via.Appreciation of Beauty & Excellence: You notice the beauty and excellence around you; you are often awe-struck by beauty, greatness, and/or the moral goodness you witness; you are often filled with wonder.
via.Bravery: You face your fears and overcome challenges and adversity; you stand up for what is right; you do not shrink in the face of pain or inner tension or turmoil.
via.Creativity: You are viewed as a creative person; you see, do, and/or create things that are of use; you think of unique ways to solve problems and be productive.
via.Curiosity: You are an explorer; you seek novelty; you are interested in new activities, ideas, and people; you are open to new experiences.
via.Fairness: You believe strongly in an equal and just opportunity for all; you don't let personal feelings bias your decisions about others; you treat people the way you want to be treated.
via.Forgiveness: You readily let go of hurt after you are wronged; you give people a second chance; you are not vengeful or resentful; you accept people's shortcomings.
via.Gratitude: You regularly experience and express thankfulness; you don't take the good things that happen in your life for granted; you tend to feel blessed in many circumstances.
via.Honesty: You are a person of high integrity and authenticity; you tell the truth, even when it hurts; you present yourself to others in a sincere way; you take responsibility for your actions.
via.Hope: You are optimistic, expecting the best to happen; you believe in and work toward a positive future; you can think of many pathways to reach your goals.
via.Humility: You let your accomplishments speak for themselves; you see your own goodness but prefer to focus the attention on others; you do not see yourself as more special than others; you admit your imperfections.
via.Humor: You are playful; you love to make people smile and laugh; your sense of humor helps you connect closely to others; you brighten gloomy situations with fun and/or jokes.
via.Judgment: You are analytical; you examine things from all sides; you do not jump to conclusions, but instead attempt to weigh all the evidence when making decisions.
via.Kindness: You do good things for people; you help and care for others; you are generous and giving; you are compassionate.
via.Leadership: You positively influence those you lead; you prefer to lead than to follow; you are very good at organizing and taking charge for the collective benefit of the group.
via.Love: You are warm and genuine to others; you not only share but are open to receiving love from others; you value growing close and intimate with others.
via.Love of Learning: You often find ways to deepen your knowledge and experiences; you regularly look for new opportunities to learn; you are passionate about building knowledge.
via.Perseverance: You keep going and going when you have a goal in mind; you attempt to overcome all obstacles; you finish what you start.
via.Perspective: You take the 'big picture' view of things; others turn to you for wise advice; you help others make sense of the world; you learn from your mistakes.
via.Prudence: You are wisely cautious; you are planful and conscientious; you are careful to not take undue risks or do things you might later regret.
via.Self-Regulation: You are a very disciplined person; you manage your vices and bad habits; you stay calm and cool under pressure; you manage your impulses and emotions.
via.Social Intelligence: You pay close attention to social nuances and the emotions of others; you have good insight into what makes people 'tick'; you seem to know what to say and do in any social situation.
via.Spirituality: You hold a set of beliefs, whether religious or not, about how your life is part of something bigger and more meaningful; those beliefs shape your behavior and provide a sense of comfort, understanding, and purpose.
via.Teamwork: You are a collaborative and participative member on groups and teams; you are loyal to your group; you feel a strong sense of duty to your group; you always do your share.
via.Zest: You are enthusiastic toward life; you are highly energetic and activated; you use your energy to the fullest degree.
